days.title: Delhi to Guptkashi (450 kms / 14–16 hrs) | Overnight Journey
activities: The group assembles at the designated pickup point in Delhi by evening.
We begin our overnight journey to Guptkashi, a sacred Himalayan town nestled in the Mandakini river valley and a key stop en route to Kedarnath.
The drive takes us through spiritually rich towns like Haridwar, Devprayag (where the Alaknanda and Bhagirathi rivers merge), Srinagar, and Rudraprayag.
Sit back, relax, and let the mountains pull you in.
days.highlight: Scenic overnight drive through sacred confluences and Himalayan foothills

days.title: Arrival in Guptkashi | Rest & Acclimatization
activities: After the long journey, arrive in Guptkashi by afternoon.
Check into your hotel and freshen up.
This day is reserved for rest and acclimatization, which is essential before beginning the high-altitude trek to Kedarnath.
You may take a short stroll around the town or visit nearby temples and local markets in the evening
Guptkashi is known for its ancient Vishwanath Temple and the Ardhnareshwar Temple —optional if you're up for light exploration.
Overnight stay in Guptkashi
days.highlight: Easy-paced day to recover and acclimatize amidst serene Himalayan surroundings

days.title: Guptkashi to Kedarnath (30 km drive + 16 km trek)
activities: Begin your day early with a drive to Gaurikund, the base point of the Kedarnath trek.
From here, start the 16 km trek to Kedarnath, one of the most sacred shrines dedicated to Lord Shiva. The trail takes you through pine forests, waterfalls, and panoramic mountain views.
Trek options include pony, palki (palanquin), or helicopter services at an additional cost.
Reach Kedarnath by late afternoon or evening and check into your hotel or guesthouse.
Attend the mesmerizing evening aarti at Kedarnath Temple, a deeply spiritual moment under starlit skies.
Overnight stay in Kedarnath
days.highlight: Trek to Kedarnath and attend the spiritually moving evening aarti

days.title: Kedarnath Darshan | Trek Down to Guptkashi
activities: Wake up early for morning darshan at Kedarnath Temple if you missed it the previous evening.
After breakfast, begin your descent to Gaurikund and drive back to Guptkashi.
This day allows you to rest and recover from the trek, soaking in everything you've experienced so far.
Spend your evening at leisure or reflecting on your spiritual journey
Overnight stay in Guptkashi
days.highlight: Return from Kedarnath with calm energy and time to rest

days.title: Guptkashi to Badrinath (190 kms / 7–8 hrs)
activities: Post breakfast, begin your drive to Badrinath, another important stop in the Char Dham circuit, located at an elevation of 10,170 ft.
The route is incredibly scenic, taking you through Joshimath, a town known for its spiritual energy and confluence points.
En route, admire the majestic Himalayas, alpine villages, and the beauty of the Alaknanda river valley.
Check into your hotel in Badrinath and relax after the journey.
Overnight stay in Badrinath
days.highlight: Serene mountain drive to the sacred town of Badrinath

days.title: Badrinath Sightseeing | Mana Village Exploration | Drive to Rishikesh
activities: Begin your day early with a darshan at the Badrinath Temple, dedicated to Lord Vishnu.

Post temple visit, head to Mana — India’s last village near the Tibetan border. Explore spiritual sites like: Bhim Pul – a natural bridge formed over the Saraswati River, Vyas Gufa – where Ved Vyas is believed to have written the Mahabharata and Saraswati River Origin – a rare sight of the mythological river’s emergence
After lunch, begin your return drive to Rishikesh, crossing scenic landscapes and confluences.
Arrive in Rishikesh by late evening and check in to your hotel.
Overnight stay in Rishikesh
days.highlight: Badrinath darshan and exploring mythological legends at Mana Village

days.title: Rishikesh Sightseeing | Drive to Delhi (240 kms / 6–7 hrs)
activities: After breakfast, soak in the spiritual and calming vibe of Rishikesh — the yoga capital of the world.
Visit iconic attractions: Ram Jhula & Laxman Jhula – suspension bridges with sweeping river views and Triveni Ghat – participate in or witness the soul-stirring morning Ganga Aarti
Post sightseeing, begin your drive back to Delhi.
Tour Ends

days.title: Delhi – Haridwar (230 km / 6–7 hrs) | Altitude: 314 m
activities: On arrival at Delhi Airport or Railway Station, meet our representative and begin your drive to Haridwar, a sacred gateway to the Char Dham Yatra.
Upon reaching, check in to your hotel and take some time to relax.
If time allows, you may explore Mansa Devi Temple, Chandi Devi Temple, and Daksha Mahadev Temple,each with its own spiritual significance.
In the evening, witness the soul-stirring Ganga Aarti at Har Ki Pauri, where devotees gather to offer floating diyas to the holy Ganges in a mesmerizing and heartfelt ritual.
Return to your hotel after the aarti.
Overnight Stay at Haridwar
days.highlight: Visit to Har Ki Pauri and Ganga Aarti

days.title: Haridwar to Uttarkashi (200 kms / 5–6 hrs)
activities: After breakfast, begin your scenic drive from Haridwar to Uttarkashi, located on the banks of the Bhagirathi River.
On arrival, check into your hotel and relax. Later, visit the revered Vishwanath Temple, one of the oldest Shiva temples in the region, and explore nearby spiritual sites if time permits.
The remainder of the day is free to unwind and acclimate for the journey ahead.
Overnight Stay at Uttarkashi
days.highlight: Visit to Vishwanath Temple in Uttarkashi

days.title: Uttarkashi – Gangotri – Uttarkashi (100 kms / 3–4 hrs each side)
activities: Early morning, depart for Gangotri, one of the most sacred temples dedicated to Goddess Ganga.
En route, take a holy dip in the Garam Kund at Gangnani, a natural thermal spring.
Continue the drive through the picturesque Harsil Valley, renowned for its deodar forests and stunning Himalayan landscapes.
Upon arrival at Gangotri Temple (3048 MTS), take a sacred dip in the Bhagirathi River, believed to be the origin of the Ganga.
Offer prayers and perform pooja rituals at the temple, and spend time soaking in the peaceful surroundings.
Later, return to Uttarkashi by evening.
Overnight Stay at Uttarkashi
days.highlight: Darshan at Gangotri Temple and dip in the Bhagirathi River

days.title: Uttarkashi to Guptkashi (220 kms / 8–9 hrs)
activities: After an early breakfast, begin your drive from Uttarkashi to Guptkashi, passing through Moolgarh and Lambgoan.
En route, witness the serene flow of the Mandakini River at Tilwara, which originates from the Kedarnath glacier.
Enjoy the scenic route as you follow the river upstream, soaking in the views of lush valleys and rolling hills.
Upon reaching Guptkashi, check in to your hotel and relax.
In the evening, visit the Ardh Narishwar Temple, a unique shrine symbolizing the union of Lord Shiva and Goddess Parvati.
Overnight Stay at Guptkashi
days.highlight: Scenic drive along Mandakini River and visit to Ardh Narishwar Temple

days.title: Guptkashi to Kedarnath (30 kms by road + 19 kms trek)
activities: Early morning, drive to Sonprayag/Sitapur, followed by a local jeep transfer to Gaurikund (at own cost).
From Gaurikund, begin the 19 km uphill trek to Kedarnath (3584 MTS). The trek can be completed on foot, by pony, or doli (at own cost).
En route, witness the changing terrain as you ascend through alpine forests, waterfalls, and rugged mountain paths.
On reaching Kedarnath, check in to your accommodation and take some time to rest.
Visit the sacred Kedarnath Temple, one of the twelve Jyotirlingas and an important pillar of the Char Dham Yatra.
Overnight Stay at Kedarnath
days.highlight: Trek to Kedarnath and visit Kedarnath Temple

days.title: Kedarnath to Guptkashi (19 kms down trek + 30 kms / 1 hr drive)
activities: Rise early before dawn and visit Kedarnath Temple by 4:45 AM to attend the Abhishek ceremony. Devotees may enter the Garbha Griha, touch the idol, and offer prayers.
After darshan, begin the 19 km downhill trek to Gaurikund.
From Gaurikund, take a local jeep (at own cost) to Sonprayag, followed by a drive to Guptkashi.
On arrival, check into your hotel and relax after the long journey.
Overnight Stay at Guptkashi
days.highlight: Early morning Abhishek at Kedarnath Temple

days.title: Guptkashi to Badrinath (215 kms / approx. 7 hrs)
activities: After breakfast, begin your scenic drive to Badrinath via Joshimath, a key town on the Char Dham route.
Upon arrival, check in to your hotel and unwind.
In the evening, visit the sacred Badrinath Temple and attend the Aarti, surrounded by the serene Himalayan ambience.
Overnight Stay at Badrinath
days.highlight: Evening Aarti at Badrinath Temple

days.title: Badrinath to Rudraprayag (160 kms) / Kirtinagar (200 kms)
activities: Begin your day with a holy dip in the Tapt Kund, a natural hot spring believed to purify devotees before temple darshan.
Have Darshan at Badrivishal, the grand statue of Lord Vishnu and a sacred symbol of Badrinath.
Visit Brahamakapal, a holy site where Pinddan rituals for ancestors are performed.
Explore nearby spiritual sites within 3 km like Mana Village, the last Indian village before the Indo-Tibet border, steeped in mythology.
Vyas Gufa, the cave where Sage Vyas is said to have composed the Mahabharata.
Maatamoorti, a revered statue depicting Goddess Durga in fierce form.
Charanpaduka, sacred footprints believed to be those of Lord Rama.
Bhimkund, a deep natural water tank surrounded by legends of Bhima from Mahabharata.
Mukh of Saraswati River, the mysterious origin point of the sacred Saraswati River. Later, drive back via Joshimath and reach Rudraprayag/Kirtinagar.
Check in to your hotel and relax for the night.
Overnight Stay at Rudraprayag or Kirtinagar
days.highlight: Darshan at Badrivishal and visit to Mana Village & nearby sites

days.title: Rudraprayag to Rishikesh (140 kms / 4–5 hrs) | Kirtinagar to Rishikesh (110 kms)
activities: After breakfast, drive to Rishikesh, a serene spiritual town nestled along the Ganges and surrounded by the Shivalik hills.
Upon arrival, check into your hotel.
Later, visit these famous spiritual landmarks like Laxman Jhulla, iconic suspension bridge linked to the legend of Laxman, Lord Rama’s brother.
Ram Jhulla, another famous suspension bridge connecting spiritual hubs on the Ganges.
Bharat Mandir, one of the oldest temples in Rishikesh dedicated to Lord Vishnu.
Shivananda Ashram, a peaceful spiritual retreat founded by Swami Shivananda.
In the evening, experience the divine Ganga Aarti at Parmarth Ashram, renowned for its nightly spiritual gatherings.
Overnight Stay at Rishikesh
days.highlight: Ganga Aarti at Parmarth Ashram

days.title: Rishikesh – Haridwar – Delhi (250 kms / 6–7 hrs)
activities: After breakfast, begin your return journey from Rishikesh to Delhi, passing through Haridwar.
Enjoy the scenic drive along the plains and foothills as you reflect on the spiritual experiences of your Yatra.
On arrival in Delhi, you will be dropped at the railway station or airport for your onward journey.
We hope you return with blessings, memories, and inner peace.

days.title: Delhi to Barkot (460 kms / 10 hrs) | Overnight Journey
activities: The group assembles at the pickup point in Delhi
We begin our overnight journey to Barkot, a scenic hill town nestled in the foothills of the Himalayas and the gateway to Yamunotri.
We will be crossing Haridwar and Mussoorie on our way.
Sit back and enjoy the drive as the hustle of the city slowly fades.

days.title: Arrival in Barkot | Rest & Acclimatization
activities: After a long overnight journey from Delhi, you will arrive at Barkot by early afternoon. This is the base for your upcoming Yamunotri trek.
Upon arrival, check into your hotel and freshen up. The rest of the day is reserved for rest and acclimatization, allowing your body to adjust to the altitude and mountainous terrain.
You can spend your leisure time relaxing at the hotel, enjoying the fresh mountain air, or taking a short walk around the peaceful town.
This easy-paced day is important to recharge before the physically demanding trek to Yamunotri, one of the four sacred Char Dham sites.
Overnight stay at Barkot
days.highlight: Leisure day in Barkot

days.title: Barkot – Yamunotri – Barkot (38 kms drive + 6 kms trek one way)
activities: Early morning, drive to Janki Chatti, the base point for the Yamunotri trek. The scenic drive takes you through winding mountain roads surrounded by pine forests and distant snow-covered peaks.
Visit Surya Kund, a natural hot spring where pilgrims boil rice wrapped in cloth as an offering. The cooked rice is taken back as prasad.
Offer prayers at Divya Shila, a sacred rock pillar located near the temple entrance, believed to be touched before entering the main shrine.
After completing your rituals and soaking in the serene vibes of the Himalayas, trek back to Janki Chatti and begin your return drive to Barkot.
Overnight stay in Barkot

days.title: Barkot to Uttarkashi (90 kms / 4 hrs)
activities: After breakfast, check out from the hotel and drive towards Uttarkashi, a town known for its spiritual significance and serene surroundings.
On arrival, visit the prominent temples like Vishwanath Temple, dedicated to Lord Shiva and one of the most revered shrines in the region
Post temple visit, check into your hotel.
The rest of the day is free to relax and acclimatize for the next day of the journey.You can also explore local markets.
Overnight Stay at Uttarkashi
days.highlight: Visit to Vishwanath Temple and Shakti Temple

days.title: Uttarkashi – Gangotri – Uttarkashi (100 kms / 3–4 hrs each way)
activities: Start your day early as you drive towards Gangotri, one of the most revered Char Dham sites dedicated to Maa Ganga, the goddess of purity and salvation.

Upon reaching Gangotri, pay your respects at the Gangotri Temple, a pristine white structure that stands gracefully along the banks of the Bhagirathi River. The temple is believed to mark the origin point of the sacred River Ganga.
Darshan of Maa Ganga at the temple, where you can witness pilgrims offering prayers and performing rituals in the lap of the Himalayas.
Visit Bhagirath Shila, a stone slab near the temple where King Bhagirath is believed to have meditated to bring the Ganga down to Earth.
Explore Gauri Kund and Surya Kund, natural water formations nearby, where pilgrims often take holy dips and admire the crystal-clear glacial waters gushing through the rocks.
After spending peaceful moments at this spiritual site and soaking in the divine atmosphere, begin your return journey to Uttarkashi by late afternoon.
Overnight Stay at Uttarkashi
days.highlight: Darshan of Maa Ganga at Gangotri Temple and visit to sacred sites like Bhagirath Shila and Surya Kund.

days.title: Uttarkashi to Guptkashi (220 kms / 8–9 hrs)
activities: Begin your day early with a long but visually rewarding drive from Uttarkashi to Guptkashi.
The route takes you via Rudraprayag, one of the five sacred confluences (Panch Prayag) where the Alaknanda and Mandakini rivers merge, forming a powerful spiritual and natural spectacle.
Along the way, you’ll also pass Devprayag, the revered confluence of the Bhagirathi and Alaknanda rivers
Upon arrival in Guptkashi check into your hotel and relax after the long journey.
Overnight stay in Guptkashi
days.highlight: Scenic drive through the river confluences of Devprayag and Rudraprayag, and arrival at the holy town of Guptkashi.

days.title: Guptkashi to Kedarnath (30 kms by road + 19 kms trek)
activities: Begin your day early with a short drive to Gaurikund, the base point for the sacred trek to Kedarnath. From here, begin your 16 km uphill trek to one of the holiest shrines in India – Kedarnath Temple, dedicated to Lord Shiva.
The route winds through stunning landscapes of dense forests, cascading waterfalls, and snow-capped peaks.
You can choose to trek on foot or opt for pony, palki (palanquin), or helicopter services (additional cost).
On arrival in Kedarnath by afternoon/evening, check in to your hotel or guesthouse and relax.
Attend the evening aarti at the Kedarnath Temple, a deeply spiritual experience surrounded by mighty Himalayan silence
Overnight stay in Kedarnath
days.highlight: Trek to Kedarnath and witness the divine evening darshan

days.title: Guptkashi to Badrinath (215 kms / 7 hrs)
activities: If you missed the darshan the previous day, wake up early for morning darshan at Kedarnath Temple, enjoying the serene Himalayan sunrise.
Begin your trek down to Gaurikund after breakfast.
From Gaurikund, continue your return drive to Guptkashi.
After a long day, check into your hotel and rest.
Overnight stay in Guptkashi
days.highlight: Peaceful return from Kedarnath with time to rest.

days.title: Badrinath to Rishikesh (300kms / 9 hrs)
activities: After breakfast, begin your drive to Badrinath, the final Dham in the Char Dham Yatra, via the scenic mountain town of Joshimath.
En route, stop at Narsingh Temple, dedicated to Lord Vishnu’s incarnation and believed to be connected to the prophecy of the last Badrinath darshan.
Also witness the majestic Vishnuprayag, the confluence of Alaknanda and Dhauliganga rivers.
Upon reaching Badrinath, check into your hotel and relax for the evening.
Overnight stay in Badrinath
days.highlight: Spiritual drive through sacred confluences and arrival at Badrinath.

days.title: Badrinath Darshan | Mana Village Visit | Transfer to Rishikesh
activities: Start your day with an early morning darshan at Badrinath Temple, one of the holiest shrines dedicated to Lord Vishnu and part of both the Char Dham and Chota Char Dham circuits.
After darshan, visit Mana Village, known as India’s last village before the Tibetan border.

Explore key spots, Bhim Pul – a natural stone bridge over the Saraswati River, Vyas Gufa – where Ved Vyas is believed to have composed the Mahabharata, Saraswati River Origin – a rare glimpse of the mythological river
Post sightseeing, begin your drive to Rishikesh. Arrive by late evening and check into your hotel.
Overnight stay in Rishikesh
days.highlight: Badrinath Temple darshan and exploring the spiritual legends of Mana Village.

days.title: Rishikesh Sightseeing | Ganga Aarti Experience
activities: Spend the day exploring the spiritual and laid-back vibe of Rishikesh, nestled on the banks of the Ganga and surrounded by the Himalayas.

Visit iconic landmarks like: Ram Jhula & Laxman Jhula – iconic suspension bridges with panoramic views. Triveni Ghat – witness the soulful Ganga Aarti in the evening ● Relax by the Ganga ghats or unwind at one of the town’s many riverside cafés with views and serene vibes.
Overnight journey to Delhi
days.highlight: Evening Ganga Aarti at Triveni Ghat and soulful exploration of Rishikesh.

days.title: Rishikesh → Delhi Drop
activities: Arrive by morning with divine memories of the Char Dham Yatra and the Himalayan spiritual circuit.
Tour Ends

days.title: Delhi to Guptkashi (450 kms / 14–16 hrs) | Overnight Journey
activities: The group assembles in Delhi by evening for pickup in a private vehicle.
We begin our overnight journey to Guptkashi, a sacred town nestled in the Mandakini River valley and the gateway to Kedarnath.

As we move away from the city's bustle, the journey offers scenic views of the holy Ganga, crossing spiritual towns like Devprayag and Rudraprayag, where sacred river confluences (prayags) amplify the energy of the Himalayas.
days.highlight: Spiritual night drive through the river valleys of Uttarakhand Note: This is a personal/private tour

days.title: Arrival in Guptkashi | Leisure & Acclimatization
activities: Arrive in Guptkashi by afternoon or early evening after the long journey.
Check in to your hotel and relax to recover from travel fatigue.
Guptkashi is a peaceful town surrounded by snow-covered peaks and known for its mythological connection to Lord Shiva.
You can explore the quiet charm of the local markets or take a short walk around the surroundings for acclimatization.
Overnight stay in Guptkashi
days.highlight: Rest and unwind amidst serene Himalayan settings before the Kedarnath trek

days.title: Guptkashi to Kedarnath (30 km drive + 16 km trek)
activities: Start your day early with a short drive to Gaurikund, the base point for the sacred trek to Kedarnath.
From Gaurikund, begin your 16 km uphill journey through pine forests, waterfalls, and majestic Himalayan vistas.
Trek options include pony, palki (palanquin), or helicopter services (at extra cost) for those who prefer alternatives.
Arrive in Kedarnath by late afternoon or evening and check into your guesthouse or hotel.
If time permits, attend the evening darshan at Kedarnath Temple. You can also opt to visit Bhairavnath Temple via a short trek.
Overnight stay in Kedarnath
days.highlight: Begin your spiritual journey with the revered Kedarnath trek and temple visit Weather Note: Nights at Kedarnath are cold – carry adequate warm clothing, medications, and essentials

days.title: Kedarnath Darshan | Trek Down to Guptkashi
activities: Wake up early for morning darshan at Kedarnath Temple if you missed it the previous evening.
Soak in the divine energy of the temple nestled against the dramatic backdrop of snow-covered peaks.
Post darshan, begin your trek down to Gaurikund and drive back to Guptkashi.
Check into your hotel and spend the evening relaxing and reflecting on the experience.
Overnight stay in Guptkashi
days.highlight: Spiritual closure of Kedarnath visit and peaceful return to base

days.title: Guptkashi to Rishikesh (210 kms / 7–8 hrs)
activities: After breakfast, check out and begin your drive to Rishikesh — the yoga capital of the world and a hub of spirituality, music, and serene energy.
Arrive by afternoon or early evening and check into your hotel.

Spend your evening exploring Rishikesh’s top spiritual landmarks: Ram Jhula & Laxman Jhula – iconic suspension bridges over the Ganga, Triveni Ghat – attend the powerful and moving Ganga Aarti and Parmarth Niketan Ashram – one of the oldest and most serene ashrams on the riverbank
Wrap up your day unwinding by the ghats or at a riverside café.
Overnight stay in Rishikesh
days.highlight: Ganga Aarti experience and soaking in the spiritual charm of Rishikesh

days.title: Rishikesh Exploration | Return to Delhi (240 kms / 6–7 hrs)
activities: Enjoy a relaxed morning in Rishikesh. After breakfast, you can visit: Beatles Ashram – known for its graffiti art and tranquil vibe (optional)
After sightseeing, begin your return journey to Delhi.
Tour Ends

days.title: Delhi to Mussoorie
activities: You will be picked up from Delhi Railway station /Airport and reach Mussoorie (7 Hrs).
Then reach by evening and check in at Hotel and enjoy evening on Mall Road.
Dinner & overnight stay at Hotel.
days.highlight: Mall road

days.title: Mussoorie Sightseeing
activities: After breakfast, you will go for Mussoorie local sightseeing.
Youl will visit Kempty Falls, Mussoorie Lake, Mossy Falls, Bhatta Falls & Company Garden.
Then, you will have dinner and stay overnight in Mussoorie.
Highlights of the Day: Kempty Falls, Mussoorie Lake, Mossy Falls, Bhatta Falls & Company Garden

days.title: Mussoorie to Dhanaulti
activities: Dhanaulti is a small town near Mussoorie, beautifully nestled amid the lofty Himalayan peaks. It does not have any grand attractions, but small and beautiful places that offer solitude and peace, close to nature.
After Breakfast drive to Dhanaulti and check in at camps after rest enjoy great sightseeing.
You will visit Jain Temples, ECO-Park, Dashavatar Temple.
Then you will have dinner & overnight stay at hotel in Dhanaulti.
days.highlight: Jain Temples, ECO-Park, Dashavatar Temple

days.title: Dhanaulti to Delhi Departure
activities: Head towards Delhi for departure.
